item.title: Age of Capricorn (21'810—19'650BC)
item.description: The Age of Capricon is the first house that we’re going to look at. Using the nominal period of 25'920 years for the precessional Year, we can determine the duration of one precessional Month to be exactly 2'190 years. That is the period of time durng which the rising Sun on the Vernal Equinox faces a given Zodiacal house on the ecliptic. The Age of Capricorn is therefore the precessional Month or the period of time during which the Sun rises in the Zodiacal image of the Capricorn constellation in its background during Spring Equinox.

item.title: Age of Sagittarius (19'650—17'490BC)
item.description: Wheel of Heaven is a knowledge base around the working theory that life on Earth was intelligently designed by an extraterrestrial civilization, the so-called Elohim.

item.title: Age of Scorpio (17'490—15'330BC)
item.description: Wheel of Heaven is a knowledge base around the working theory that life on Earth was intelligently designed by an extraterrestrial civilization, the so-called Elohim.

item.description: Wheel of Heaven is a knowledge base around the working theory that life on Earth was intelligently designed by an extraterrestrial civilization, the so-called Elohim.
